name: EBT — Energy-Based Transformer with Closed-Form Quadratic Refinement (Notable Non-Record, negative result)
author: alexdwu13
config: 8xH100 SXM5, 600s wallclock, sp4096, 9L/448D, K_train=K_eval=8, energy_rank=32, eta_init=0.05, aux=0.3, h0_noise=0.05

baseline_for_comparison.name: baseline_v3 (matched 9L/448D, EBT off, fixed DDP path)

iso_step_seed1337.interpretation: EBT loses 0.0022 BPB even when both run the same number of optimizer steps with identical warmdown. Architecture per-step quality is worse, not just wallclock-cost.

compute_trade_seed1337.interpretation: Smaller-backbone-plus-refinement compute-trade fails: the lost backbone capacity dominates. Would need a much larger refinement effect to recover.

k_eval_ablation_3seed_mean.interpretation: Monotonic K_0 to K_train across all seeds and configurations (also reproduced at iso-step and on 8L/384D); refinement is doing real test-time compute. K=16 over-refines and diverges.

headline_finding: Negative result. EBT v4 trails the matched 9L/448D no-EBT baseline by 0.0044 BPB on wallclock and by 0.0022 BPB at iso-step on 8xH100 SXM5 / 600s / 16MB. K_eval ablation is monotonic in every config (K=0->K=8 improves val_bpb by 0.0003-0.0009), so refinement is doing real test-time compute -- it just doesn't pay back its 11% per-step cost or substitute for backbone capacity at this scale.
engineering_notes: First EBT submission to openai/parameter-golf (0/1867 PRs match keyword scan).
Closed-form quadratic energy g(h) = 0.5||A h||^2 + b.h with analytic gradient AᵀA h + b. Compatible with torch.compile(fullgraph=True) and mx.compile -- no autograd nesting required.
PyTorch and MLX backends are byte-for-byte the same math; MLX used for local smoke testing only.

DDP find_unused_parameters trap: an earlier baseline kept the EBT params present but unused, with find_unused_parameters=True. That added ~15% per-step overhead and gave a clean false-positive 'EBT wins by 0.0014 BPB' result. Fix is to skip constructing the EBT params entirely when REFINE_STEPS_TRAIN=REFINE_STEPS_EVAL=0 and AUX_LOSS_WEIGHT=0; baseline goes from 82 ms/step to 64 ms/step on 8xH100 SXM5.

smoke_mlx.config: NUM_LAYERS=2 MODEL_DIM=128 ENERGY_RANK=32 K_train/K_eval variable ITERATIONS=1000 TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=512 TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS=4096

ablation.variant: Trained EBT, refinement OFF at eval

smoke_mlx.note: Disabling refinement at eval after training with it (+0.034 bpb worse than the never-refined baseline) was the original signal that the refinement loop is doing real test-time work. Confirmed at scale by the 8xH100 K_eval ablation.

notes: Non-record submission. 16MB artifact-size and SOTA statistical-significance criteria do not apply; targets the 'unique and interesting approaches' bar including interesting negative results. Headline 3-seed wallclock train logs are train_seed{1337,42,2026}.log (ebt_v4); the matched 3-seed baseline numbers are summarized in results.csv. Diagnostic runs are diag_iso_step_baseline.log, diag_iso_step_ebt.log, and diag_compute_trade.log.
